data.title: Supreme Court declines to block suit against banks on muni price-fixing
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court allowed a class action lawsuit against banks, including JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Bank of America (BAC), to proceed by declining to take up the banks' appeal.

The court, without explanation, declined to take up the appeal of a lower court's decision to certify the case as a class action, which allows a group of plaintiffs to consolidate their cases into one proceeding. A group of municipalities, led by the city of Philadelphia, is alleging that banks inflated interest rates on state and municipal bonds.

The other defendants in the suit are units of Citigroup (C), Morgan Stanley (MS), Goldman Sachs Group (GS), Barclays (BCS), Royal Bank of Canada (RY), and Wells Fargo (WFC).

The case could eventually settle for ~$770M, with Bank of America (BAC) and JPMorgan (JPM) having the most at stake, Bloomberg News reported, citing Bloomberg Intelligence litigation analysts.

In 2019, the city of Philadelphia sued a group of seven banks, alleging that they conspired to inflate pricing of floating-rate bonds for municipalities from as early as 2008. The class action suit added an eighth bank.

In their appeal to the Supreme Court, the banks said the municipalities hadn't demonstrated that issues that all plaintiffs have in common will predominate over individual questions. Therefore, they hadn't met one of the class action requirements.

In Monday morning trading, bank stocks were mixed. Bank of America (BAC) stock fell 0.8%, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) stock gained 1.4%, Citi (C) rose 0.1%, Morgan Stanley (MS) rose 0.8%, Goldman Sachs (GS) edged up 0.3%, Barclays (BCS) fell 1.1%,  RBC (RY) increased 0.7%, and Wells Fargo (WFC) advanced 0.4%.

data.title: 3M Q1 preview: Oil costs, soft consumer demand in focus
data.description:
3M (MMM) is set to announce first-quarter earnings on Tuesday, and investors will watch out for the impact of elevated oil prices on the company’s products, which use oil-based polymers.

Additionally, investors will also keep an eye on its consumer products and consumer electronics businesses amid weakened consumer sentiment.

Wall Street expects the maker of Post-it Notes, Scotch tape, and Command strips to post EPS of $1.98, implying a 5.3% increase, while revenue is seen rising 3.4% to $6B.

During its Q4 2025 earnings call, the company projected approximately 3% organic sales growth for 2026, operating margin expansion of 70 to 80 basis points, and EPS of $8.50 to $8.70.

However, CEO William Brown warned about elevated oil prices due to ongoing conflict in the Middle East as well as weaker consumer sentiment during the JPMorgan Industrials Conference in March 2026, while reiterating the 3% organic growth target.

RBC Capital Markets maintained its Underperform rating for MMM while increasing its price target to $134 from $125.

The brokerage firm highlighted that consumer electronics and broader consumer weakness are likely to disappoint on growth this quarter and for the remainder of the year. Additionally, PFAS personal injury litigation remains an overhang.

RBC Capital analyst Deane Dray noted that the management has tamped down expectations, citing incremental consumer electronics softness (and memory pricing) and weakened consumer sentiment (energy cost concerns), though industrial orders remain solid.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating and Wall Street analysts are bullish, rating  3M a Buy. On the other hand, Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious, rating MMM a Hold.

However, Seeking Alpha analyst Steven Fiorillo rated the stock as a Buy, saying that 3M Company is transitioning from a litigation-driven narrative to an execution-focused story as major PFAS liabilities are now resolved and structured.

Fiorillo added that the management, under CEO Bill Brown, is accelerating innovation, targeting 350 new product launches in 2026, and focusing on high-margin growth while divesting low-margin segments.

Over the last two years, MMM has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 63% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen three upward revisions, compared to five downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen one upward revision against five downward moves.

Shares of 3M are down more than 4% year to date, underperforming the benchmark S&P 500, which has gained over 4% in the same period.

data.title: Docusign, Klaviyo and Paycom are Barclays' favorite software stocks going into Q1
data.description:
Docusign (DOCU), Klaviyo (KVO), and Paycom (PAYC) are Barclays' favorite software stocks going into earnings season, the investment firm said on Monday. 

“It is the seasonally smallest Q, macro was not a tailwind, which is also reflected in our mixed checks,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. “In other words, we need company-specific stories to get excited – here we like DOCN, KVYO and PAYC. The MSFT setup is tough.”

For Docusign, the first quarter is not likely to be better-than-expected, but it is a period in which management can discuss the company's future growth outlook after the recent capital raise, the analysts said.

“We believe the much better balance sheet position (we estimate net debt + leases / adj. EBITDAR to drop to ~0.9x in Q1, from 3.2x in Q4), and the combination of leasing and CapEx that management is utilizing to drive the capacity build-out, could enable a much stronger growth in outlook. Supporting our view is our updated DOCN model with new bottom-up, capacity driven revenue, CapEx and lease builds that factor in the investment requirements and unit economics of DOCN's AI infrastructure buildout.” 

For Klaviyo, the analysts said they are seeing “more resilience” in the front office, especially after Braze's (BRZE) “healthy” results a couple of weeks ago. 

“We expect another healthy quarter from KVYO, with growth close to 30%, which should confirm that the modern Customer Engagement Platforms can continue to thrive in this new AI world,” the analysts added.

For Paycom, the analysts said the reduction in headcount “offers room for PAYC to beat on EBITDA meaningfully.“

data.title: European natural gas surges up to 11% as Iran shuts Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
European natural gas futures soared as much as 11% Monday after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. seized an Iranian ship, dimming hopes for a near-term diplomatic resolution in the Middle East war.

Shipping traffic through Hormuz halted over the weekend after vessels came under fire in the waterway and Iran warned against crossings; several LNG tankers loaded with fuel from Qatar approached the strait, but ultimately U-turned or idled without making the journey.

The benchmark Dutch TTF front-month contract recently traded up 4.7% to €$40.60/MWh after tumbling Friday on news that passage through the strait was open to commercial traffic.

Europe will have to compete for liquefied natural gas with Asia, which also needs to offset lost volumes from Qatar, according to Rystad Energy's VP of gas and LNG markets Masanori Odaka.

"Two things can happen: TTF and JKM spread can narrow, or European demand destruction progresses even more," Odaka told Bloomberg, referring to the price difference between the pricing benchmarks in Europe and Asia.

Yet while Europe continues to compete with Asia for LNG cargoes, relatively subdued demand from Asia has helped ease pressure on European supply, according to analysts from ANZ Bank.

ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Choice Hotels integrates AI across hotel ecosystem with AWS
data.description:
• Choice Hotels (CHH) is integrating artificial intelligence across its operations in collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AMZN) to assist guests in booking hotels, help franchisees with revenue management and guest communication, and enable the company to optimize and manage its inventory of hotels. 
  • By integrating AI across its full hospitality chain, Choice Hotels (CHH) can deploy “a more intelligent, responsive, and efficient enterprise built on AWS cloud infrastructure.”
  • “Choice Hotels is a powerful example of what it looks like when an enterprise truly commits to AI at scale,” said Rich Geraffo, vice president and managing director of AWS North America.

data.title: Hormuz disruption lingers: Markets price gradual reopening, not a quick fix
data.description:
Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has effectively stalled Monday after a brief and disjointed reopening over the weekend collapsed, highlighting the fragility of efforts to restore flows through one of the world’s most critical energy corridors.

The disruption intensified following the first U.S. seizure of an Iranian-linked vessel, a development that has heightened uncertainty for operators and insurers navigating the region. As a result, market participants are increasingly turning to prediction markets for guidance on when conditions may normalize. 

Current pricing suggests only a 24% chance that traffic returns to typical levels before May 1, with probabilities rising to roughly 40% by mid-May. The gradual increase implies expectations for a prolonged, uneven recovery rather than a swift resumption of activity.

  • Before May 1, 2026 — 24% chance.
  • Before May 15, 2026 — 40% chance.
  • Before Jun 1, 2026 — 60% chance.
  • Before Jul 1, 2026 — 70% chance.
  • Before Aug 1, 2026 — 77% chance. 
  • Before Sep 1, 2026 — 79% chance.
  • Before Oct 1, 2026 — 80% chance. 
  • Before Jan 1, 2027 — 86% chance. 
  • Before Apr 1, 2027 — 92% chance. 
  • Before Jul 1, 2027 — 94% chance. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: The controlling owner of Brown-Forman prefers Pernod as a buyer over Sazerac
data.description:
Brown-Forman (BF.A) (BF.B) was one of the leading gainers in the S&P 500 Index in early action on Monday after Bloomberg reported that the family that owns the company is in favor of a sale to French distiller Pernod Ricard SA (PDRDF) over an alternative proposal from Sazerac Co.

Sources indicate that the family sees Pernod (PDRDF) as the better fit because the combined company would be diversified across geographies and types of spirits. In addition, the proposed terms for a merger with Pernod would give the family a bigger stake in the business and more influence than if Sazerac was the acquirer. Brown-Forman (BF.A) (BF.B) is controlled by the Brown family, descendants of founder George Garvin Brown. They own roughly half to more than 70% of the company’s voting shares.

Notably, Brown-Forman (BF.A) (BF.B) and Pernod (PDRDF) are in discussion about keeping Louisville a key region for the combined entity. The deal being bandied about would be 80% in stock and 20% in cash.

A merger between the two spirits powerhouses would combine Sazerac labels such as Buffalo Trace, Blanton’s, Weller, Eagle Rare, Fireball, Southern Comfort, and RTD brand BuzzBallz with Brown-Forman (BF.A) (BF.B) Brands including Jack Daniel’s, Woodford Reserve, Old Forester, Herradura tequila, and el Jimador tequila.

data.title: Full Truck Alliance on positive Catalyst Watch for earnings growth—Citi Research
data.description:
Citi Research added Full Truck Alliance (YMM) to its 30-day Catalyst Watch with a Buy recommendation as the company now shows better earnings growth visibility.

The earnings potential reflects the company’s dominance in a “well-established ecosystem,” a ramp-up in commission revenue that should drive the company’s sales and earnings growth, and penetration in the less-than-truckload and intra-city transportation market that “shows promise,” Citi’s Brian Gong noted.

However, Gong still assigns a “High Risk” rating to the stock given its short trading history and the ongoing data investigation into the company. This investigation could result in material fines and a protracted suspension of new user registrations.

Additionally, a slow ramp-up of commissions and the need to offer large-scale incentives in the intra-city transportation market are also potential risks to the stock’s upside.

data.title: Wall Street slides as hopes of U.S.-Iran peace deal fade
data.description:
Wall Street traded lower on Monday as optimism over a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal faded, with escalating tensions in the Middle East weighing on investor sentiment and prompting a pullback in equities following last week’s rally.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slipped -0.2%, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) dipped -0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) hovered near even. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, six of the 11 S&P segments are trading up in the green, with energy at the top as oil (CL1:COM) was up over 4%. At the other end of the spectrum, consumer discretionary has suffered the most so far in the session. 

Iran will not participate in a second round of talks with the United States, according to Iranian state media, which said Tehran objected to what it described as excessive and unrealistic demands from Washington.

U.S. Treasury yields were slightly higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.25%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) climbed 2 basis points to 3.72%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was mostly unchanged at 4.88%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Marvell Technology (MRVL) climbed +4.9%, while shares of Intel (INTC) dropped -3.2%.
